items.title: The Persistence of Homophobia in Men’s Friendship Norms
items.author: Sherrie Y. Xue, Stephanie C. Lin, Christilene du Plessis
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Across five studies and one supplementary study (five preregistered;N= 3,215 adults), we found that men, more than women, avoided shared experiences (e.g., going to the movies, sharing food) with individuals of the same gender. items.title: Public Speakers With Nonnative Accents Garner Less Engagement

items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Can nonnative English accents become barriers to garnering attention in public discourse? The current study examined this question. items.title: Do the Effects of a Preschool Language Intervention Last in the Long Run? A 4-Year Follow-Up Study
items.author: Åste Mjelve Hagen, Kristin Rogde, Monica Melby-Lervåg, Arne Lervåg
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Childhood language interventions appear promising for improving children’s lives and yielding economic returns.
